methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $2.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 18 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $900M - $8.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 36.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Axiom Space, a Series G space infrastructure company founded by Michael T. Suffredini and Kam Ghaffarian, carries a current post-money valuation of $2.5B following $350M in total capital raised. The valuation sits within a competitive peer set of 18 same-sector companies spanning both public equities (5 tickers including RKLB, PL, LUNR) and late-stage privates, with comparable valuations ranging from $900M to $8.0B.

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a standard 1.25x Series G step-up multiplier to the $2.5B base, implies $3.1B. Peer-median analysis across the 18-company comparable set yields $1.6B, with sample reference points including Astranis at $3.5B (Series D), Relativity Space at $4.2B (private), and Sierra Space at $8.0B (Series C). A public-comp approach using the 36.9x P/S median for Space & Aerospace cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure.

The triangulated range spans $1.4B (low) to $3.6B (high) with a median of $2.4B, supporting the current $2.5B valuation near the midpoint. Key uncertainties center on revenue trajectory and unit economics relative to the broader peer cohort, as well as the wide dispersion within the comparable set—a 9x spread from floor to ceiling—reflecting meaningful heterogeneity in business model and development stage within commercial space infrastructure.
